title: Movements in Time
editorialSummary: Art movements grouped by century of origin. The existing pool has 'Art Movements' as a label (puzzle-0114) listing Cubism, Surrealism, Baroque, Minimalism — these labels are distinct (17th-Century Movements, 18th-Century Movements, Late 19th-Century Movements, Early 20th-Century Movements). False trail: Rococo and Neoclassicism are both 18th-century and correctly grouped together; Baroque is 17th-century and belongs in its own group despite sounding similar. Note: Romanticism is excluded to avoid the early/late ambiguity — Post-Impressionism anchors the late 19th-century group.

meta.gptRationale: Art-movement chronology plus specialized labels like Veduta and Tenebrism make this noticeably trickier than a typical medium-tier sort.
